articles.title: Oil Companies’ Huge Profits Revive Calls for Temporary Windfall Taxes
articles.summary: European nations imposed temporary taxes in the 2022 energy shock when Russia invaded Ukraine, but whether they can effectively help households is up for debate.

articles.title: Congress ends record shutdown at the Department of Homeland Security
articles.summary: Thursday's vote in the House provides funding for DHS after a more than two-month shutdown,  but does not include dollars for Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Customs and Border Protection.

articles.title: Takeaways From Hegseth’s Second Day of Testimony on the Iran War
articles.summary: The secretary was also questioned over civilian deaths, an accusation of antisemitic remarks and women in combat.

articles.title: Trump gives the go-ahead for a major new Canada-U.S. oil pipeline

articles.title: Trump’s Plans to Boost Weapons Production Might Not Deliver for Years
articles.summary: While the defense industry has announced plans to make more munitions, much of that expanded production will not quickly kick in.

articles.title: Here’s What States Might Do After the Voting Rights Decision
articles.summary: The effect of the Supreme Court’s ruling could be as little as one House seat in Louisiana in 2026, but pressure is building on Tennessee and South Carolina Republicans to act.

articles.title: Hegseth Says Iran Cease-Fire Stops Clock for Congressional Approval
articles.summary: The defense secretary testified on the eve of the 60-day mark of the war, a major statutory deadline for the president to withdraw forces or seek approval from Congress to continue the fight.

articles.title: 20,000 civilian sailors are stuck in the gulf that opens to the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Thousands of seafarers remain stuck as the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues. The United Nations urges for a plan to facilitate their release.

articles.title: What Peak Gerrymandering Could Look Like Now
articles.summary: The redrawing of America’s congressional districts is sure to escalate after the Supreme Court’s decision, with some maps that would have seemed laughable a year ago.

articles.title: Will.i.am wants to future-proof a new generation
articles.summary: The Black Eyed Peas co-founder turned entrepreneur is now teaching a class on "agentic AI" for Arizona State.

articles.title: Supreme Court Voting Rights Ruling Could Fuel New Era of Redistricting Wars
articles.summary: The expected flood of new congressional maps is likely to produce fewer competitive districts, fewer ways for voters to hold elected officials accountable and more polarized politics.

articles.title: Trump pulls Casey Means' stalled surgeon general nomination, announces new pick

articles.title: U.S. House primaries in Louisiana are suspended after Voting Rights Act ruling
articles.summary: Louisiana suspended its upcoming primaries for the U.S. House, following Wednesday's U.S. Supreme Court ruling that the state's congressional map is an "unconstitutional racial gerrymander."

articles.title: How Conservatives on the Supreme Court Weakened the Voting Rights Act
articles.summary: The Supreme Court just overturned Louisiana’s congressional voting map, landing the latest blow to the landmark Voting Rights Act. Abbie VanSickle, a reporter covering the court for The New York Times

articles.title: In real-world test, an AI model did better than ER doctors at diagnosing patients
articles.summary: Researchers evaluated how well an AI model could diagnose and make decisions about patient care.

articles.title: The Onion's bid to take over Infowars moves to the Texas Supreme Court
articles.summary: The state's highest court will now consider a deal that would allow the Onion to license the Infowars brand name and turn the show into a mockery of itself.

articles.title: Myanmar junta says Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, doubts linger
articles.summary: The secrecy surrounding Suu Kyi's new location has alarmed her son, Kim Aris, who says he has received no authoritative confirmation of her wellbeing.

articles.title: U.K. calls antisemitism an emergency after arson and stabbing attacks on Jewish people
articles.summary: The British government pledged to increase security for Jewish communities after a string of arson attacks and a double stabbing. But members of the community lashed out at the government.

articles.title: Iran war has cost $25B, Pentagon says. And, SCOTUS strikes blow to Voting Rights Act
articles.summary: The Pentagon estimates the war with Iran has cost $25 billion so far. And, the Supreme Court ruled that Louisiana's 2024 election map was "an unconstitutional racial gerrymander."

articles.title: Trump says he spoke with Putin about a possible ceasefire in Ukraine
articles.summary: President Trump said he talked to Russian President Putin Wednesday about the wars in Ukraine and Iran.

articles.title: Trump says he is weighing reducing American troop presence in Germany after Iran feud
articles.summary: President Trump suggested he could soon reduce the U.S. military presence in Germany as he continues to feud with Chancellor Friedrich Merz over the U.S-Israel war against Iran.
